best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-authentication-002 title: user logs in unsuccessfully due to incorrect password module: authentication source pages: 2 preconditions: user has registered an account. test data: - email: test@example.com - password: wrongpassword test steps: 1. user initiates login request to the application. 2. application forwards the request to the user management system. 3. user management system checks if the user is logged in. 4. user management system requests firebase authentication api to get the current user. 5. firebase authentication api returns null as the user does not exist. 6. user management system confirms the user is not logged in and prompts the user to try again. 7. application displays an error message. expected result: user is prompted to try logging in again due to incorrect password.

test case id: tc-calendarmanagement-002 title: display null calendar module: calendar management source pages: 5, 6 preconditions: no calendar data is available. test data: - no specific input required. test steps: 1. application requests calendar data from the calendar management system. 2. calendar management system forwards the request to the data management system. 3. data management system queries the database for the calendar data. 4. database returns null as there is no calendar data. 5. data management system forwards null to the calendar management system. 6. calendar management system forwards null to the application. 7. application displays null. expected result: application displays null as there is no calendar data.

test case id: tc-calendarmanagement-001 title: set and display calendar module: calendar management source pages: 5, 6 preconditions: calendar data is available. test data: - calendar data: {id: 1, date: "2023-10-01", events: ["event1", "event2"]} test steps: 1. application sends calendar data to the calendar management system. 2. calendar management system forwards the calendar data to the data management system. 3. data management system queries the database for the new calendar data. 4. database returns the status code indicating success. 5. data management system forwards the outcome to the calendar management system. 6. calendar management system forwards the outcome to the application. 7. application displays the calendar data. expected result: application displays the calendar data sent by the user.
